source_verification_method.result: Both methods failed to retrieve prize table content. Official Taiwan Lottery pages are JavaScript single-page applications (SPAs). WebFetch returns navigation shell only — no prize tables rendered. Chrome MCP was unavailable (not connected). All three pages confirmed as MANUAL_VERIFICATION_REQUIRED.
source_verification_method.fallback_source: Internal repo documentation: lottery_api/CLAUDE.md (calc_prize docstrings at lines 976-1023) and lottery_api/calculate_win_probability.py (DAILY_539 prize tiers at lines 65-70). These documents the same prize tier logic embedded in the production calc_prize function.

POWER_LOTTO.note: Official page is a JavaScript SPA; prize table not machine-readable. Internal source documents the calc_prize function used in production.

BIG_LOTTO.note: Official page is a JavaScript SPA; prize table not machine-readable. Internal source documents the calc_prize function used in production.

DAILY_539.note: Official page is a JavaScript SPA; prize table not machine-readable. Internal source documents the prize structure used in the repo probability calculator.

POWER_LOTTO.pool_structure: First zone: pick 6 from 1-38. Second zone (特別號/special): pick 1 from 1-8.
POWER_LOTTO.special_hit_logic: special_hit = (predicted_special == actual_special) — exact match of predicted second-zone number against actual second-zone draw
POWER_LOTTO.schema_limitation: predicted_special is only populated for ~25% of POWER_LOTTO replay rows (approx 9,000 of 36,104). Second-zone-dependent tiers cannot be fully evaluated on the remaining ~75% of rows.

BIG_LOTTO.pool_structure: Pick 6 main numbers from 1-49. Special number drawn from remaining 43 numbers.
BIG_LOTTO.special_hit_logic: special_hit = (actual_special IN predicted_numbers) — the actual special number appears among the 6 predicted main numbers
BIG_LOTTO.schema_limitation: actual_special is 100% populated for BIG_LOTTO (24,140 rows). special_hit is always computable.

DAILY_539.pool_structure: Pick 5 main numbers from 1-39. No special number.
DAILY_539.special_hit_logic: No special number exists. special_hit is always 0 for DAILY_539.
DAILY_539.schema_limitation: actual_special is 0 for all DAILY_539 rows (confirmed). No second-zone logic applicable.

checked_endpoints.verified_coverage: Covers all 10 POWER_LOTTO prize tiers: tiers with hit_count>=3 (貳/肆/陸/柒/玖/參/伍/頭 if special) plus hit_count>=1 AND special_hit=1 (普獎 1+1, 捌獎 2+1, and all higher special tiers)
checked_endpoints.notes: Endpoint correctly captures all prize-winning conditions. No over-inclusion (non-prize rows excluded). Schema limitation on predicted_special applies to ~75% of rows — those rows cannot evaluate special_hit-dependent tiers.

checked_endpoints.verified_coverage: Covers all 8 BIG_LOTTO prize tiers: tiers with hit_count>=3 (all 7 upper tiers) plus 普獎(2+1)
checked_endpoints.notes: Endpoint is exact. 100% of BIG_LOTTO rows can evaluate special_hit since actual_special is fully populated.

checked_endpoints.verified_coverage: Covers all 4 DAILY_539 prize tiers: 肆獎(2), 參獎(3), 貳獎(4), 頭獎(5)
checked_endpoints.notes: Simple main-number-only condition. No special number dimension. Exact match with verified prize structure.

POWER_LOTTO.note: POWER_MAIN_M3_PLUS_DIAGNOSTIC misses 捌獎 (2+1) and 普獎 (1+1). ANY_PRIZE_AWARE_WIN adds those.

BIG_LOTTO.note: BIG_MAIN_M3_PLUS_DIAGNOSTIC misses 普獎 (2+1). ANY_PRIZE_AWARE_WIN adds that tier.

DAILY_539.note: D539_M3_PLUS_DIAGNOSTIC misses 肆獎 (hit_count=2). ANY_PRIZE_AWARE_WIN adds that tier.

design_principles: Pure functions only — no DB access, no global state, no mutation
Stateless — same inputs always produce same outputs
No dependency on replay pipeline or M3+ scoring
No production API integration in this skeleton phase
Read-only evaluation tool — never writes results to DB
Parallel track — does not replace, override, or change M3+ scoring

proposed_functions.name: classify_power_lotto_tier
proposed_functions.note: Raises ValueError if inputs are out of valid range

proposed_functions.name: classify_tier
proposed_functions.signature: classify_tier(lottery_type: str, hit_count: int, special_hit: int) -> str
proposed_functions.returns: tier_class string, dispatches to game-specific classifier
proposed_functions.note: Primary entry point. Raises ValueError for unknown lottery_type.

proposed_functions.name: is_any_prize_aware_win
proposed_functions.signature: is_any_prize_aware_win(lottery_type: str, hit_count: int, special_hit: int) -> bool
proposed_functions.returns: True if tier_class is not NO_PRIZE, False otherwise

proposed_functions.name: score_replay_row
proposed_functions.signature: score_replay_row(lottery_type: str, hit_count: int, special_hit: int) -> dict
proposed_functions.returns: {tier_class: str, is_prize_aware_win: bool, is_m3_plus: bool, endpoint_flags: dict}
proposed_functions.note: Returns both prize-aware and M3+ evaluation for the same row. M3+ is included as a parallel diagnostic, not replaced.

scoring_engine_design.future_phase_boundary: P271C may implement this skeleton. Connecting it to replay evaluation (batch scoring) requires separate P271C authorization.

hit_count.range: 0 to max_pick_count (5 for DAILY_539, 6 for BIG_LOTTO/POWER_LOTTO)

special_hit.validation: Raise ValueError if not 0 or 1. For DAILY_539, must always be 0.
special_hit.note: For POWER_LOTTO rows where predicted_special is NULL, special_hit is 0. Caller must handle NULL → 0 mapping upstream.

input_contract.schema_limitation_note: POWER_LOTTO predicted_special is only populated for ~25% of rows. Caller is responsible for signaling this limitation. score_replay_row does NOT access DB to resolve NULL values.

is_prize_aware_win.semantics: True if tier_class is not NO_PRIZE

is_m3_plus.semantics: True if hit_count >= 3. Parallel diagnostic — P265A SSOT definition, special_hit excluded.

endpoint_flags.note: Computed boolean flags per endpoint definition from P271A

output_contract.coexistence_guarantee: is_m3_plus is ALWAYS included in output alongside is_prize_aware_win. The two metrics are parallel and independent. Neither replaces the other.

POWER_LOTTO.note: 0 main hits is never a prize even with special

BIG_LOTTO.note: 頭獎: special irrelevant when hit_count=6

parallel_feature_design.principle: Prize-aware scoring is a NEW PARALLEL feature track alongside existing M3+/replay scoring. It does NOT replace, deprecate, or modify the existing M3+ endpoint or any replay infrastructure.

existing_track_preserved.status: UNCHANGED — all existing tests, API contracts, DB queries, and research pre-registered against M3+ continue to use M3+ without modification
consumers_protected: all pre-registered hypothesis entries using M3+ as endpoint

new_parallel_track.implementation_mode: Future pure-function module (P271C). No new columns written to strategy_prediction_replays, no schema change, no existing query modified.
new_parallel_track.coexistence_guarantee: Prize-aware rates are reported as ADDITIONAL metrics alongside M3+ rates, never as substitutes. Any future report that includes prize-aware rates MUST also report the corresponding M3+ rate for the same cell.
new_parallel_track.promotion_gate: Prize-aware endpoints are evaluation tools only. No strategy promotion, registry write, or production deployment is authorized based solely on prize-aware rates.

parallel_feature_design.no_migration_path: There is no migration path from M3+ to prize-aware. Both tracks run in perpetuity. Deprecating M3+ requires a separate explicit governance decision with user authorization.

p271c_stop_conditions: If P271C implementation writes to any DB table — STOP
If P271C implementation modifies any existing replay query — STOP
If P271C implementation changes M3+ metric semantics — STOP
If P271C implementation adds columns to strategy_prediction_replays — STOP
If P271C implementation is connected to any recommendation or strategy pipeline — STOP
If P271C implementation claims hit-rate improvement — STOP
If P271C implementation removes or deprecates existing M3+ endpoint — STOP
If P271C is started before explicit user authorization — STOP
forbidden_actions: No backtest was run in P271B
No replay evaluation was run in P271B
No DB write happened in P271B
No hypothesis_registry.jsonl mutation happened in P271B
No strategy was generated in P271B
No hit-rate improvement is claimed in P271B
Existing M3+/replay scoring semantics were not changed
Replacement/migration of M3+ was not authorized
No production/API/frontend scoring runtime code was modified
limitations: Official Taiwan Lottery pages are JavaScript SPAs — prize tables cannot be machine-read via WebFetch or curl. Source status is MANUAL_VERIFICATION_REQUIRED for all three lotteries.
Prize amounts (NTD) are not included — official pages required manual browser access which was unavailable in this session.
POWER_LOTTO predicted_special is only ~25% populated (9,000 of 36,104 rows). Second-zone-dependent tier classification cannot be fully evaluated for the remaining ~75% of rows.
Prize-aware scoring engine (prize_aware_scorer.py) is a DESIGN ARTIFACT only. No Python module was implemented in P271B. Implementation is deferred to P271C with separate explicit authorization.
This task is specification/design only. It does not constitute betting advice, a guaranteed win strategy, or an approved prediction method.
next_recommended_task: HOLD / WAITING_FOR_USER_AUTHORIZATION. If user authorizes P271C: implement prize_aware_scorer.py as a pure-function module under lottery_api/research/prize_aware_scoring/ with the unit_test_fixture_matrix defined here. P271C must not modify existing scoring code, must not write to DB, and must include coexistence tests confirming M3+ is unchanged.
